findings.summary: Config-dependent compound chain: under --yes-always, indirect injection from scraped/file content can drive an auto-approved package install (pip runs code at install) or an out-of-repo file write with no explicit-confirm gate, giving external-content-to-code-execution in one hop.

Untrusted local content (file contents, code comments, commit messages, and AI!/AI? watch comments) enters the LLM context in coders/base_coder.py with no origin labeling or injection neutralization, making every read file an indirect prompt-injection surface.

Web scraping via /web and auto-detected URLs (commands.py cmd_web, base_coder.check_for_urls) injects raw remote page content into the LLM context unlabeled as untrusted, and the add-URL confirm gate is auto-approved under --yes, creating a remote indirect-injection ingress.

File writes are not confined to the repository root: allowed_to_edit/abs_root_path join paths without verifying the result stays under root, so absolute paths and ../ traversal reach the host filesystem, gated only by a generic confirmation that --yes-always auto-approves (remit forbids out-of-repo writes outright).

Confirmation-bypass: --yes/--yes-always auto-approves package installs, self-upgrade (network code fetch), out-of-repo file creation, and URL scraping because those confirm_ask gates omit explicit_yes_required; only LLM-suggested shell commands (handle_shell_commands) are protected by explicit confirmation.

Self-upgrade and install-from-main-branch (versioncheck.py) fetch and execute code from the network (git+https://github.com/Aider-AI/aider.git and PyPI, self_update=True), an autonomous-code-provenance surface behind a single confirm prompt that --yes bypasses.

PostHog telemetry is initialized with enable_exception_autocapture=True (analytics.py), which can transmit exception tracebacks containing file paths, code context, and argument values to a third-party endpoint, exceeding the remit boundary of anonymous aggregate metrics with no code/PII.

No secret detection or redaction: file contents (including a .env or credential file if added to the chat) and edits/commit messages flow into the LLM context and into git with no secret scanning, so the remit's secret-redaction and never-commit-secrets intent is unimplemented.

No structured action-level audit log: aider records only human-readable chat/LLM/input transcripts (.aider.*.history), with no durable structured record of approvals, shell executions, git operations, or out-of-repo access — an oversight gap over consequential capabilities.

The interactive /help RAG feature (help.py) builds and persists a llama_index VectorStoreIndex with HuggingFace embeddings under ~/.aider/caches, injecting retrieved docs into the LLM context; the on-disk embedding cache has no integrity verification, so a tampered cache could inject behavior-altering content (ingest source is aider's own bundled docs, limiting exposure).

No evidence of adversarial or prompt-injection red-team testing driving the agent's trust model; the extensive tests/ suite is functional/regression testing, not adversarial evaluation of the untrusted-content and confirmation-gate surfaces.
